A '''security spike''' or '''slicing spike''' was an electronic device used to bypass electronic security measures, usually to open the electronic lock of a door or container. It was similar to the more advanced [[Computer spike/Legends|computer spike]]. Many security spikes could only be used once.<ref name="KOTOR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Security spikes first appeared in [[BioWare]]'s [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', where they were intended to be single-use items that would give a +5 bonus to the player character's roll when attempting to unlock doors or containers. Due to a bug in the code, security spikes did not function correctly in the [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] release of ''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic''. This bug was not present on the original Xbox physical copy, and was fixed for both later releases of the game<ref name="KOTOR" /> and the game's 2004 sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''.<ref name="TSL">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>

They can be made by [[Mission Vao]] aboard the [[Ebon Hawk]] for free.<ref name="KOTOR" />
